name: Lacasa Of Goshen Inc - Branch Office
description_short: Non-profit housing and financial empowerment organization serving Elkhart County since 1970, offering affordable housing, financial education, and counseling services.
description_long:
Lacasa of Goshen Inc was founded in 1970 by area church leaders to address the need for safe, affordable housing for migrant workers. Over 50 years, the organization evolved to serve all residents of Elkhart County, becoming a major community asset with approximately 400 affordable rental units and deep roots in local churches and community partnerships. Today, Lacasa operates as Elkhart County's primary organization for individual, family, and community empowerment.

Lacasa offers a comprehensive range of housing and financial services. Their full-service Financial Empowerment Center provides classes in Financial Education and Homebuyer Education, matched savings accounts, individual debt reduction counseling, and goal-setting support. Housing services include rental apartment assistance, homebuyer programs, and the Help-a-House program for low-income homeowners needing repairs or weatherization work. They maintain multi-family rental properties in both Elkhart and Goshen. Counseling and coaching services are available in both English and Spanish.

The organization distinguishes itself through its long-term community commitment, non-profit status, and integrated approach combining housing solutions with financial literacy. Unlike for-profit lenders, Lacasa prioritizes resident empowerment and neighborhood vitality. Their matched savings programs (including the DreamSavers Program mentioned on their site) and counseling services address root causes of financial instability rather than charging interest on debt.

Lacasa is appropriate for Elkhart County residents seeking affordable housing, financial education, or debt counseling from a trusted non-profit. However, they do not provide emergency cash loans, credit repair services, or traditional lending products. Their services are community-focused rather than designed for rapid credit score improvement or immediate liquidity needs.
pros: Over 50 years of established community presence and trust in Elkhart County
Matched savings programs (DreamSavers) that help families build assets toward homeownership
Bilingual services in English and Spanish for housing counseling and financial coaching
Comprehensive Financial Empowerment Center with debt reduction counseling and goal-setting support
Non-profit model prioritizing resident empowerment over profit extraction
Help-a-House program providing repair and weatherization assistance for low-income homeowners
Manages approximately 400 affordable rental units, ensuring access to safe housing options
cons: Limited to Elkhart County service area; cannot help residents outside this geographic region
Does not offer emergency or payday loans for immediate cash needs
No credit repair or credit building services; focuses on education and counseling rather than score improvement
Classes and events have specific scheduled dates, limiting flexibility for spontaneous enrollment
Website does not specify application timelines, eligibility criteria, or program waitlists
best_for: Elkhart County residents seeking affordable rental apartments or homeownership assistance
Low-income families wanting to build financial literacy and savings habits
Homeowners needing repair or weatherization work through the Help-a-House program
Spanish-speaking residents requiring bilingual housing counseling and financial coaching
diagnosis: Lacasa is best for Elkhart County residents seeking affordable housing, financial education, or debt counseling from a trusted, non-profit community organization. The main caveat is that they do not provide credit repair, emergency loans, or rapid credit score improvement—they focus on foundational financial empowerment and asset-building through education, counseling, and matched savings.
services: Financial literacy and 'Master Your Money' classes
Debt reduction counseling and 'Master Your Debt' classes
Housing counseling in English and Spanish
Community engagement and neighborhood vitality programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
